tagline: Work with Intercom workspace data from the command line.

global_flags.name: connection
global_flags.summary: Use a saved Intercom connector credential.

commands.summary: View one contact by ID or external ID
commands.notes: Planned for #169 as a bounded direct read over GET /contacts/{contact_id} and GET /contacts/find_by_external_id/{external_id}.
Planned for #169 as a bounded direct read over GET /conversations/{conversation_id}.

commands.summary: List public or internal Intercom articles
commands.notes: Candidate stream coverage for #167; not executable in the current five-stream bundle.
Candidate stream/body-query coverage for #167/#170; not executable in this metadata slice.
Planned for #170 with export job lifecycle and bounded output policy; no download is executed by this metadata.

commands.summary: Download a completed Intercom export with size bounds
commands.notes: Planned for #170 as a bounded binary/file read. The metadata does not expose filesystem writes or raw downloads.

commands.risk: Creates customer data in the configured Intercom workspace.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, and execute.
commands.notes: Planned for #171 with typed schemas and redaction.

commands.risk: Mutates customer data in the configured Intercom workspace.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, and execute.
commands.notes: Planned for #171 with typed schemas and redaction.

commands.risk: Deletes customer data from the configured Intercom workspace.
commands.approval: Destructive reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute, and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Blocked until #171 adds a destructive confirmation policy.

commands.risk: Sends a visible reply in an Intercom conversation.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, and execute.
commands.notes: Planned for #171 with explicit message schema and preview text.

commands.summary: Create or update an Intercom tag
commands.risk: Creates or mutates tag metadata in the configured Intercom workspace.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, and execute.

commands.risk: Deletes tag metadata from the configured Intercom workspace.
commands.approval: Destructive reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute, and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Blocked until #171 adds a destructive confirmation policy.

commands.summary: Fetch call recording metadata or bounded media
commands.notes: Planned for #170 as a bounded binary/file read with no unsafe filesystem write escape hatch.

help_topics.name: intercom-safety
help_topics.summary: Intercom writes remain blocked until typed reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, execute, and confirmation gates are implemented.
